spec_match.rationale: The monitor now uses a real local server route that records Sec-Purpose/Purpose request headers and applies a server-side speculative budget policy.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the header probe, Run server-backed wave, Fire all prefetches, and Reset controls, with rows and timeline updating from server-recorded decisions.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept and feature index copy now describe server-backed budgeting for link rel=prefetch requests rather than a client-only simulation.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers slim speculative responses, a logical over-budget block with servedKb 0, and a plain fetch path that receives a full response without Sec-Purpose.

references.rationale: The page retains the ChromeStatus feature 6247959677108224 link and Fetch spec reference.

design_system.rationale: The repaired status badges and budget UI use shared CSS variables, explicit button colors, and no inline event handlers.

summary: Re-reviewed in chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome 148.0.0.0. Before the fix, /v138/pass-sec-purpose-prefetch-header-with-link-rel-prefetch/prefetch-budget-monitor/?verify=before-budget-route-fix claimed Sec-Purpose support after a plain fetch to the page and generated the budget timeline entirely in client state. After the fix, /prefetch-budget-monitor/?verify=after-budget-route-fix-clean observed Sec-Purpose: prefetch on a local <link rel=prefetch> probe. Run server-backed wave recorded five real budget-endpoint prefetch requests with Sec-Purpose: prefetch, slim decisions of 18 KB, 20 KB, 25 KB, and 15 KB, one logical blocked decision at 63/80 KB, and a plain fetch full response of 42 KB with no Sec-Purpose header. DevTools request inspection confirmed sec-purpose: prefetch on the link-prefetch request and no Sec-Purpose on the plain fetch. Reset restored the queue and server budget. The updated conformance route /v138/pass-sec-purpose-prefetch-header-with-link-rel-prefetch/conformance/?verify=after-budget-route-fix-clean reported 8 PASS / 0 FAIL. Console was empty and network requests were clean with successful page, CSS, font, favicon, and budget-endpoint requests.
